items.title: Couple forced to move home after being built on wrong block

children.content: A Victorian couple has been forced to move their home that was accidentally built on the wrong block.

items.title: Homicide detectives investigating death of man in Box Hill apartment tower

children.content: Emergency services attended a residential tower on Whitehorse Road in Box Hill early this morning following reports of an altercation.

image.altText: Forensic services arriving at a Box Hill tower.

items.title: Egg-laying hens in cages set for better living conditions under new laws

children.content: The lives of caged hens are set to improve as Victoria implements new welfare laws, but farmers say they have not been consulted.

image.altText: A hen inside a cage inside a poultry farm

items.title: How a dog fence in Coburg ended in conflict resolution workshops

children.content: A controversial fence designed to keep dogs out of a park in Melbourne's north has been removed after community opposition.

gallery.altText: The words stop the fence spray painted on a tree.

thumbnail.altText: The words stop the fence spray painted on a tree.

items.title: Police failures exposed during inquest into 50 year cold case

children.content: Ms Green was last seen alive on February 13, 1976, with the Coroner's Court of Victoria hearing the litany of policing errors following her disappearance.

image.altText: A black and white photo of Veronica Green who wears a long white dress.

items.title: Driver seriously hurt in truck crash that shut Melbourne freeway for hours

children.content: The crash shutdown outbound lanes of the Princes Freeway at Officer in Melbourne's south-east.

image.altText: An aerial photo of fire trucks, ambulances and emergency workers surrounding two crashed trucks on a freeway on a sunny day.

items.title: Reefs at risk as warming water sees sea urchins spread further

children.content: Millions of destructive sea urchins are spreading from NSW into Victoria and Tasmania and the chair of a senate inquiry says reefs are at risk unless there is decisive action.

gallery.altText: A scuba diver collecting black urchins across the white sea floor.
A group of sea urchins, one with its red teeth sticking out.
diver under water with sea urchins

items.title: Police probe alleged punch that left country footy player with broken jaw

children.content: Victoria Police is investigating an on-field incident during a Good Friday football clash in the Macedon Ranges that left one player with a broken jaw and sparked a melee.

image.altText: A fight between two football teams

items.title: Body found on rural Victorian property during search for missing man

children.content: Police have discovered the body of a man on a property east of Ouyen in regional Victoria.

image.altText: image of richard

items.title: Tourist hotspots share how fuel crisis impacted long weekend visitors

children.content: It was a mixed weekend for many tourism businesses across the country, amid fears that rising fuel costs would keep people home. For some, it was business as usual, but fuel uncertainty has many in the sector feeling nervous about the months ahead.

image.altText: Four children sit on a rug in a grassy field, with flowers in the foreground.

items.title: Farmers angered by plans to turn coal mine into lake bigger than Sydney Harbour

children.content: There are plans to turn the former Hazelwood coal mine in Victoria into a lake bigger than Sydney Harbour, but farmers and environmentalists are worried about the impact on nearby waterways.

gallery.altText: A young man with a beard, wearing a blue shirt and cap stands alongside a riverbed.

thumbnail.altText: A young man with a beard, wearing a blue shirt and cap stands alongside a riverbed.

items.title: SA premier wants 'certainty' beyond a one-year Gather Round deal

children.content: The South Australian premier is pushing for the state to be awarded a long-term contract for Gather Round, but says it could take weeks before a deal about the future of the footy festival is struck.

image.altText: Both men smiling in front of media microphones at a playground

items.title: Former coach says AFL 'shamed' him over $20K fine

children.content: Ex-Port Adelaide coach Ken Hinkley, who was fined for sledging in 2024, says he felt exploited by the league's later use of footage of his theatrics as a promotional tool.

image.altText: Coach celebrates with players, staff, in front of fans, at full-time of an AFL match

items.title: Mother of eight killed in Easter crash remembered as 'the light of many'

children.content: The family of a Victorian grandmother killed in a head-on crash south of  Brisbane remember her as "selfless".

image.altText: Roza Abebwa a woman smiling holding a bouquet.

items.title: Council workers stop collecting rubbish across Melbourne over wage dispute

children.content: Thousands of wheelie bins in several Melbourne council areas were left unemptied on Tuesday morning as workers took industrial action over pay.

image.altText: A sign on a fence saying council workers deserve fair pay.

items.title: Cyclist clinging to reeds, pulled to safety in remote Snowy River rescue

children.content: The cyclist was clinging to reeds in the middle of the Snowy River when the police air wing pulled them to safety.

image.altText: Black and white image of a cyclist in a river

items.title: CFA firefighters 'really angry, frustrated' at Easter break-in

children.content: CFA firefighters are "gutted" after thousands of dollars of equipment and fuel were stolen from Terip Terip station over the Easter long weekend.

image.altText: A woman in her 30s looks to her left. She is wearing a blue singlet with black dust and soot on it.

items.title: Voss says struggling Carlton focused to 'get on with the task'

children.content: Carlton coach Michael Voss insists he is ignoring most of the intense scrutiny on his embattled AFL club, adamant key stakeholders remain on the same page.

image.altText: Carlton Blues head coach Michael Voss leads the team off the field

items.title: Ballarat's unique approach to tackling family violence

children.content: Ballarat's approach targets causes of domestic and family violence as rates remain high.

gallery.altText: A wall with little photos of women's faces on it.

thumbnail.altText: A wall with little photos of women's faces on it.

items.title: Blues recruit's striking ban upheld by AFL tribunal

children.content: Carlton will be without Will Hayward for a high-stakes clash against Adelaide after failing to downgrade his one-match ban for striking.

image.altText: AFL players come together in a scuffle on the field, pulling jerseys and pushing

items.title: Hidden costs mean many comedians make 'zero profit' from festival runs

children.content: Doing a run of shows at the Melbourne International Comedy Festival is the pinnacle for a lot of Australian comedians, but the festival's tens of thousands of punters are rarely privy to the behind-the-scenes costs.

image.altText: Oliver Hunter

items.title: Melbourne woman Eva Lasrini remembered as devoted mother of two

children.content: A Dandenong woman whose body was found south-west of Melbourne on Saturday has been remembered as a devoted mother who "deserved a life filled with safety, happiness, and peace".

gallery.altText: A woman smiles while holding a bouquet of flowers as her two adult daughters kiss her cheeks.

thumbnail.altText: A woman smiles while holding a bouquet of flowers as her two adult daughters kiss her cheeks.

items.title: Fire-affected towns welcome Easter visitors

children.content: Businesses in fire-affected communities of Victoria welcome holiday-makers and daytrippers, as the recovery continues.

image.altText: A smiling man in a shirt and cap leans on a farm fence

items.title: Murray River ski boat launches into tree as race puts four in hospital

children.content: Flips, spin-outs and crashes have sent four competitors to hospital as the world's fastest water ski race was held without skiers for the second straight year after fatalities in the sport in 2024.

image.altText: Dozens of people on water ski boats at a river bank, with the Murray River in the background

items.title: The 'cheese grater' bowls that fed a generation of skaters

children.content: A set of skate bowls with origins as an outlet for teens in a tough Geelong suburb, once visited by Tony Hawk, may soon be preserved long-term through heritage protection.

image.altText: A man wearing a black t-shirt holding a skateboard in front of a graffitied wall.

items.title: Battling Bombers stare down unwanted record

children.content: Essendon cannot do anything but stay galvanised as they stare down the demoralising prospect of notching a club-record 18th consecutive defeat, coach Brad Scott has said.

image.altText: Essendon Bombers players exits the field after their loss to Western Bulldogs

items.title: UFOs over the oval: The sighting school kids were told not to talk about

children.content: Flying saucers, balloons or mass hysteria? It's 60 years today since Australia's biggest UFO sighting took place at a Melbourne school. Witnesses are still demanding answers about what happened that day.

items.title: US star Sha'Carri Richardson overcomes semis scare to win Stawell Gift

children.content: American sprint star Sha'Carri Richardson has become the third woman ever to win the famous Stawell Gift from scratch.

image.altText: Sha'Carri Richardson poses after winning a sprint heat

items.title: Price crunch has some servo owners asking: 'Is it really worth doing this?'

children.content: Workers are reporting an increase in abuse from customers as service station owners say they're not making money from fuel price rises.

image.altText: External shots of a 7/11 petrol station and fuel pump with an out of order sign.

items.title: Sisters of officer killed by Dezi Freeman to sue Victoria Police

children.content: Leading Senior Constable Neal Thompson's sisters plan to launch legal action against Victoria Police over his death. They also say they felt "abandoned" by police after their brother's death.

articles.title: Two arrested as part of investigation into movements of Dezi Freeman

children.content: Two people have been arrested as police investigate the movements of Dezi Freeman, who was fatally shot by police last week after months on the run.

image.altText: Man holding phone horizontally while wearing body camera on a property that looks like a farm with mountain in the background

articles.title: Cops would not have had 'luxury' of taking Dezi Freeman alive, experts say

children.content: A former police hostage negotiator says he was sure Dezi Freeman's seven-month run from the law "was going to end in tears" and that the fugitive would never surrender.

image.altText: A black police armourned vehicle is parked next to a shipping container. A black pipe is connected between the two.

items.title: Moira Deeming wins Victorian Liberal preselection battle

children.content: The outspoken MP has officially won top spot on the party's upper-house ticket for the Western Metropolitan Region after other candidates withdrew.

image.altText: A woman with shoulder-length black hair, and wearing a purple jacket, smiles.

items.title: Bulldogs down plucky Bombers but lose two more players to injury

children.content: The Western Bulldogs hold off an Essendon fight back to triumph by 34 points, but will sweat on the fitness of Artie Jones and Tim English, who both limped off at Docklands.

image.altText: An AFL player in red and blue is kneeled over in pain

items.title: Lethlean to challenge Victorian premier in Bendigo East

children.content: Publican Andrew Lethlean will run as the Nationals candidate for the seat of Bendigo East in the upcoming state election.

image.altText: Andrew Lethlean wearing a green Nationals cap.

items.title: New research shows regional Australians move on foot more in 'walkable' towns

children.content: The humble footpath could be the answer to being more active, with new research showing regional Australians living in "walkable towns" travelled 75 minutes more on foot each week.

image.altText: Two people walking along a path near the beach in Warrnambool.

items.title: Melburnian helps Oxford end women's Boat Race drought

children.content: Competing for Oxford in the historic women's Boat Race for a fourth time, Australia's Annie Anezakis finally tastes victory.

gallery.altText: Annie Anezakis celebrates Oxford Iniveristy winning women's Boat Race.

thumbnail.altText: Annie Anezakis celebrates Oxford Iniveristy winning women's Boat Race.
